doc06.text: ATT proposes a comprehensive technical solution for the CAG tender, leveraging our expertise in cloud-hosted SaaS solutions on AWS Singapore zone. The solution includes a highly available architecture with geographically separated production and UAT environments, ensuring 99.5% system availability. Our approach aligns with CAG's requirements for security, scalability, and performance.

The proposed system architecture features a modular design with load balancing, auto-scaling, and redundant components to meet RFP requirements. We will implement OWASP-compliant security measures, including SSL/TLS encryption, RBAC authentication, and regular vulnerability assessments.

The system will be hosted on AWS SaaS platform in Singapore zone, with geographically redundant production environments and separate UAT environments. Key components include application servers, PostgreSQL databases, and storage solutions. The architecture diagram will be inserted in the final document.

We will use Incremental Agile methodology with RAD principles, employing Spring Framework and NetBeans IDE. Version control will be managed through Git, ensuring traceability and collaboration. The development process will follow a hybrid SDLC with phases including Requirements, Design, Development, Testing, UAT, and Go-Live.

The project will be delivered in 12 months, with key milestones including Requirements Analysis (2 months), Design & Development (4 months), Testing (3 months), and UAT (3 months). Detailed Gantt chart will be included in the final document.

The security design includes RBAC authentication, LDAP/AD integration, and regular security audits. Network communication will be secured with SSL/TLS, and databases will be hardened with port control and user profile management. Annual VAPT will be conducted to ensure compliance with RFP security requirements.

doc07.text: The architecture features a multi-tiered design with load balancers, application servers, and PostgreSQL databases. Redundancy is achieved through geographically separated environments and auto-scaling. The architecture diagram will be inserted in the final document.
The system will have no single point of failure, with redundant components in production and UAT environments. Failover mechanisms will ensure continuous operation during outages.
Security measures include SSL/TLS encryption, RBAC, and regular vulnerability assessments. The system will comply with OWASP ASVS and ISO/IEC 27001 standards.

Hardware specifications include dual Xeon processors, 64GB RAM, and 2TB storage for production. UAT environments will have 50% of production capacity. Network requirements include 1Gbps connectivity with redundant switches.
The system will integrate with existing CAG systems via REST APIs and message queues. Interface testing will be conducted during UAT to ensure compatibility.
We will provide R&D plans for software upgrades and hardware refreshes. The system will be designed for scalability, allowing expansion to 140MPPA capacity in future phases.

notes: Generated by Hermes on 2026-03-15. Compliance with RFP clauses 5.1, 5.2, and 5.3 confirmed.
